Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Discharge Summary for clinical domain Oncology / Complex Chronic Care.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Marathi (code=mr, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 52
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Buyers, Other
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Maharashtra
- district: Mumbai Suburban
- first_language: Marathi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 52-year-old female buyer with a matriculation education works in procurement in urban Mumbai, Maharashtra, and is married. She is a native Marathi speaker who is fluent in Hindi and leads a lifestyle that includes daily yoga and occasional consumption of festive sweets. Her cultural background is rooted in traditional Maharashtrian customs, and she resides in the Mumbai Suburban district.

Document format guide for Discharge Summary (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] DOB [[DOB|1994-06-21]] [[AGE|30]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]] | Course / advice: <concise paragraphs matching domain + persona sex/age>

MANDATORY inline entities (14 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- ABHA_ADDRESS — ABHA Address e.g. [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
- CASTE — Caste e.g. [[CASTE|OBC]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

डिस्चार्ज समरी — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Tata Memorial Hospital, Mumbai]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Anita Deshmukh]] जन्मतारीख [[DOB|1972-08-15]] [[AGE|52]] [[GENDER|Female]]
प्रवेश दिनांक [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-1025]] कक्ष [[WARD_NUMBER|B1]] खाट [[BED_NUMBER|08]]
डॉक्टर [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Nair]] | उपचार पद्धती / सल्ला: रुग्ण ही 52 वर्षांची महिला असून तिला स्थानिक पातळीवर प्रगत उजव्या स्तनाचा कर्करोग (T3N2M0) असल्याचे निदान झाले आहे. तिने निओएडज्युव्हंट केमोथेरपी पूर्ण केली असून त्यानंतर तिची मॉडिफाईड रॅडिकल मास्टेक्टॉमी करण्यात आली आहे. शस्त्रक्रियेनंतरची सुधारणा समाधानकारक असून कोणतीही तात्काळ गुंतागुंत दिसून आलेली नाही. रुग्णाला ऑन्कोलॉजी प्रोटोकॉलनुसार ॲडज्युव्हंट रेडिओथेरपी सुरू ठेवण्याचा सल्ला देण्यात आला आहे. तिला कमी चरबीयुक्त, उच्च प्रथिनेयुक्त आहार घेण्याबाबत आणि पुढील सहा आठवड्यांपर्यंत कष्टाचे काम टाळण्याबाबत समुपदेशन करण्यात आले आहे. शस्त्रक्रिया ऑन्कोलॉजी टीम आणि रेडिएशन ऑन्कोलॉजी विभागासोबत पाठपुरावा नियोजित केला आहे. रुग्ण आणि तिचा पती, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Vikram Deshmukh]], यांना जखमेची काळजी आणि संसर्गाच्या लक्षणांबाबत शिक्षित करण्यात आले आहे.

[[DISTRICT|Mumbai Suburban]]
[[PHONE_NUMBER|919876543210]]
[[ABHA_ADDRESS|anita.deshmukh@abdm]]
[[CASTE|SC]]

[[CASTE|SC]]

professional_persona: Bablu Vyankat, a diligent software tester, utilizes manual testing expertise including test case design and defect tracking with Jira, and is building automation skills with Selenium, channeling meticulous analytical mindset and collaborative communication to drive quality in fast‑paced Agile projects.
linguistic_persona: Bablu Vyankat is fluent in Marathi and Hindi, commands functional English for technical tasks, enabling clear documentation and cross‑team communication in their software testing role.
sports_persona: Bablu Vyankat is an avid weekend cricketer who pitches for a local Maharashtra Premier League club, follows the Mumbai Indians in the IPL, and enjoys cheering for FC Pune City during I‑League matches, staying fit through regular jogs in the Pune hills.
arts_persona: Bablu Vyankat immerses in Pune’s vibrant arts scene by attending Marathi folk concerts featuring Avadhoot Gupte, grooving to indie band Parvaaz, binge‑watching classic Bollywood films like Sholay and Mughal‑e‑Azam, and occasionally doodling comic strips during commute.
travel_persona: Bablu Vyankat prefers spontaneous weekend getaways to hill stations like Lonavala, Mahabaleshwar, and Goa, while meticulously planning a once‑in‑a‑lifetime backpacking trek across the Himalayas in Nepal, balancing a modest budget with a love for exploring both local culture and international cities like Bangkok and Prague.
culinary_persona: Bablu Vyankat delights in Pune’s street‑food palette, regularly devouring vada pav, spicy misal pav, and pani puri, while occasionally whipping up quick poha with mustard seeds and curry leaves at home, and they have a soft spot for buttery naan with garlic and tangy tamarind chutney.
persona: Bablu Vyankat blends a methodical tester’s precision with a zest for Pune’s street foods and weekend cricket, yet often procrastinates on health check‑ups while juggling an ever‑growing list of automation scripts.
cultural_background: Bablu hails from Pune, a vibrant urban hub in Maharashtra known for its blend of traditional Marathi culture and modern tech-driven lifestyle. Growing up in a Hindu Maratha family, he was immersed in festivals such as Ganesh Chaturthi, Diwali, and Gudi Padwa, and enjoyed the city's famous street food like vada pav and misal. The local emphasis on community, education, and entrepreneurship shaped his balanced outlook, where curiosity meets practicality.
linguistic_background: Bablu’s mother tongue is Marathi, which he reads, writes, and speaks fluently. He also has a strong command over Hindi, both spoken and written, acquired through schooling and daily interactions in Pune. English, essential for his role as a software tester, is functional; he can understand technical documentation, write test cases, and communicate with teammates in English, though his accent reflects his Marathi upbringing.
skills_and_expertise: Bablu is proficient in manual software testing, including test case design, functional testing, and defect tracking using tools like Jira. He is familiar with Agile/Scrum development processes and has basic knowledge of test automation frameworks such as Selenium with Java/Python. His strong analytical mindset ensures meticulous attention to detail, and his effective communication skills enable clear documentation and collaborative problem‑solving within cross‑functional teams.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Manual Testing", "Test Case Design", "Functional Testing", "Defect Tracking (Jira)", "Agile/Scrum Methodology", "Basic Automation (Selenium)", "Programming (Java/Python)", "Documentation & Communication", "Attention to Detail", "Team Collaboration"]
hobbies_and_interests: Bablu enjoys playing cricket with friends on weekends, attending local music concerts featuring Marathi folk and indie bands, and exploring Pune’s culinary scene by trying different street foods. He is an avid follower of tech blogs and experiments with small automation scripts as a hobby. Additionally, he likes trekking to nearby hill stations, listening to classic Bollywood movies, and volunteering in community clean‑up drives during festivals.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Cricket", "Live Music Concerts (Marathi Folk & Indie)", "Street Food Exploration", "Tech Blogging & Automation Scripting", "Trekking to Hill Stations", "Bollywood Classic Movies", "Community Clean‑up Volunteering"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Bablu aims to advance from a manual tester to a senior quality assurance engineer within the next three to five years. He plans to acquire ISTQB certification, deepen his automation skills with Selenium and API testing, and eventually lead a QA team that drives robust testing strategies for large‑scale web applications. In the long term, he envisions becoming a test architect, shaping testing frameworks and mentoring junior testers, while staying rooted in his collaborative, methodical approach.
persona_clinical_summary: A 25-year-old male from Pune, Maharashtra, who is never married, works as a tester, and has a higher secondary education. He is fluent in Marathi and Hindi, with English proficiency required for his role. His lifestyle includes a preference for street food, weekend cricket, and a sedentary work environment.

professional_persona: Gurmej Kumar, a 25‑year‑old salt‑pan mukadam, leverages their practical, structured mindset and strong oral negotiation skills to manage labor, control evaporation cycles, and secure fair prices, while their outgoing nature keeps the team cohesive despite occasional bouts of anxiety.
linguistic_persona: Gurmej Kumar, a 25‑year‑old village dweller, speaks Punjabi fluently for daily life, understands spoken Hindi enough for casual chats, but cannot read or write in either language, relying on their strong oral storytelling and negotiation abilities.
sports_persona: Gurmej Kumar, a 25‑year‑old village cricket enthusiast, plays weekly matches on the local ground, follows the Punjab Kings in the IPL, and stays fit with regular morning jogs, though occasional late‑night bhangra sessions sometimes cut into their recovery time.
arts_persona: Gurmej Kumar, a 25‑year‑old lover of Punjabi folk, often sings Gurdas Maan’s classics, dances bhangra to Diljit Dosanjh’s beats, watches Bollywood blockbusters starring Shah Rukh Khan and Alia Bhatt on the community TV, and participates in nightly storytelling circles that keep local folklore alive.
travel_persona: Gurmej Kumar, a 25‑year‑old who enjoys short weekend getaways, often visits a nearby town’s mela for shopping and street food, dreams of a pilgrimage to Varanasi for spiritual renewal, and hopes to take a group bus trip with friends once they can afford it.
culinary_persona: Gurmej Kumar, a 25‑year‑old who loves rustic cuisine, enjoys preparing makki di roti with sarson da saag seasoned with mustard seeds, ginger, and fresh spinach, relishes buttery lassi and spicy pickles made from raw mango, and often trades recipes during village gatherings.
persona: Gurmej Kumar, a 25‑year‑old salt‑pan foreman, blends practical structure with lively village camaraderie, but their frequent anxiety and occasional impulsive splurges on sweets keep life both organized and unpredictably sweet.
cultural_background: Gurmej Kumar belongs to a close‑knit Punjabi rural community in the Bathinda district of Punjab. His family has traditionally engaged in salt‑pan work, a livelihood shaped by the arid landscape of the region. The social fabric is woven around joint‑family values, seasonal festivals like Baisakhi and Lohri, and a strong sense of community responsibility. Living in a village setting, he participates in local gatherings, fairs (melas), and agricultural cycles that define daily life.
linguistic_background: His mother tongue is Punjabi, which he speaks fluently and uses for all daily communication. He has limited exposure to Hindi, primarily understanding it in oral interactions but cannot read or write either language due to his illiterate status. While he lacks formal literacy, he possesses strong oral communication skills, enabling him to convey instructions and negotiate effectively in his role as a mukadam.
skills_and_expertise: As a mukadam in salt production, Gurmej manages a team of laborers, oversees the evaporation and crystallisation processes, and ensures the maintenance of salt pans and related equipment. He has practical knowledge of water‑flow control, timing of sun exposure, and quality assessment of harvested salt. His role also involves oral bookkeeping, negotiating prices with buyers, coordinating with village authorities, and resolving worker disputes. His sociable nature enhances his ability to motivate and organize groups efficiently.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Supervising salt-pan laborers", "Overseeing salt evaporation and crystallisation", "Maintaining salt-pan infrastructure", "Managing water-flow and drying schedules", "Assessing salt quality", "Oral bookkeeping and price negotiation", "Coordinating with village authorities", "Resolving worker disputes", "Motivating teams through group activities"]
hobbies_and_interests: He enjoys participating in village cricket matches, singing Punjabi folk songs, and dancing bhangra during festivals. Gurmej often attends local melas and fairs, helps organize community events, and likes cooking traditional Punjabi dishes like makki di roti and sarson da saag. In his spare time, he watches Bollywood movies on a community television set and engages in informal storytelling sessions with friends.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Playing cricket in the village", "Singing Punjabi folk songs", "Dancing bhangra at festivals", "Attending local melas and fairs", "Cooking traditional Punjabi dishes", "Watching Bollywood movies on community TV", "Participating in storytelling circles with friends"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Despite being illiterate, Gurmej aspires to advance within the salt‑production sector by gaining practical expertise that could eventually lead to owning a small salt‑pan of his own. He aims to improve his family's economic stability, provide better education opportunities for his children, and learn basic reading‑writing skills through community adult‑literacy programs. In the long term, he hopes to become a respected community leader who can represent workers’ interests in local cooperatives.
persona_clinical_summary: A 25-year-old male from Bathinda, Punjab, currently married, works as a salt-pan mukadam with an illiterate education level in a rural setting. His primary language is Punjabi, and he frequently experiences anxiety and impulsive spending on sweets. He is employed in a physically demanding occupation involving evaporation cycles and labor management.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.

ਮਰੀਜ਼ [[PATIENT_NAME|Balwinder Singh]] [[AGE|25]] [[GENDER|Male]] ([[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]], [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|balwinder.singh@example.com]]): ਸਰ, ਮੈਨੂੰ ਪਿਛਲੇ ਦੋ ਹਫ਼ਤਿਆਂ ਤੋਂ ਖੰਘ ਹੋ ਰਹੀ ਹੈ। ਇਹ ਠੀਕ ਨਹੀਂ ਹੋ ਰਹੀ। ਮੈਨੂੰ ਬਹੁਤ ਥਕਾਵਟ ਮਹਿਸੂਸ ਹੁੰਦੀ ਹੈ ਅਤੇ ਮੇਰਾ ਕੁਝ ਭਾਰ ਘਟ ਗਿਆ ਹੈ।
ਡਾਕਟਰ [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Harpreet Kaur]] ([[HOSPITAL_NAME|Civil Hospital Bathinda]]): ਨਮਸਕਾਰ ਬਲਵਿੰਦਰ ਜੀ, ਮੈਂ ਤੁਹਾਡੀ ਚਿੰਤਾ ਸਮਝਦਾ ਹਾਂ। ਥਕਾਵਟ ਅਤੇ ਭਾਰ ਘਟਣ ਦੇ ਨਾਲ ਖੰਘ ਦੇ ਕਈ ਕਾਰਨ ਹੋ ਸਕਦੇ ਹਨ। ਮੈਨੂੰ ਕੁਝ ਸਵਾਲ ਪੁੱਛਣ ਦੀ ਲੋੜ ਹੈ। ਕੀ ਤੁਹਾਨੂੰ ਬੁਖਾਰ ਹੈ, ਖਾਸ ਕਰਕੇ ਸ਼ਾਮ ਨੂੰ?
ਮਰੀਜ਼ [[PATIENT_NAME|Balwinder Singh]]: ਹਾਂ, ਡਾਕਟਰ। ਮੈਨੂੰ ਸ਼ਾਮ ਨੂੰ ਹਲਕਾ ਬੁਖਾਰ ਹੁੰਦਾ ਹੈ, ਲਗਭਗ 99-100 ਡਿਗਰੀ। ਮੇਰੀ ਛਾਤੀ ਵਿੱਚ ਵੀ ਕਦੇ-ਕਦੇ ਭਾਰੀਪਨ ਮਹਿਸੂਸ ਹੁੰਦਾ ਹੈ।
ਡਾਕਟਰ [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Harpreet Kaur]]: ਜਾਣਕਾਰੀ ਲਈ ਧੰਨਵਾਦ। ਤੁਹਾਡੇ ਲੱਛਣਾਂ ਨੂੰ ਦੇਖਦੇ ਹੋਏ, ਸਾਨੂੰ ਨਿਸ਼ਚਿਤ ਹੋਣ ਲਈ ਕੁਝ ਟੈਸਟ ਕਰਨ ਦੀ ਲੋੜ ਹੈ। ਮੈਂ ਤੁਹਾਡੇ ਲਈ ਬਲਗਮ ਟੈਸਟ ਦੀ ਪਰਚੀ ਭੇਜ ਰਿਹਾ ਹਾਂ। ਕਿਰਪਾ ਕਰਕੇ ਨਜ਼ਦੀਕੀ ਲੈਬ ਵਿੱਚ ਜਾਓ ਅਤੇ ਇਹ ਕਰਵਾਓ। ਨਾਲ ਹੀ, ਮੈਂ ਜੋ ਦਵਾਈ ਲਿਖ ਰਿਹਾ ਹਾਂ, ਉਹ ਲੈਣਾ ਸ਼ੁਰੂ ਕਰੋ।
ਮਰੀਜ਼ [[PATIENT_NAME|Balwinder Singh]]: ਠੀਕ ਹੈ, ਡਾਕਟਰ। ਦਵਾਈ ਦਾ ਨਾਮ ਕੀ ਹੈ? ਅਤੇ ਟੈਸਟ ਲਈ ਮੈਨੂੰ ਕਿੱਥੇ ਜਾਣਾ ਚਾਹੀਦਾ ਹੈ?
ਡਾਕਟਰ [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Harpreet Kaur]]: ਦਵਾਈ ਦਾ ਨਾਮ ਆਈਸੋਨੀਆਜ਼ਿਡ, ਰਿਫੈਂਪਿਸਿਨ, ਪਾਈਰਾਜ਼ੀਨਾਮਾਈਡ ਅਤੇ ਇਥਾਮਬਿਊਟੋਲ ਹੈ। ਇਹ ਤਪਦਿਕ (ਟਿਊਬਰਕਲੋਸਿਸ) ਲਈ ਇੱਕ ਮਿਆਰੀ ਸੁਮੇਲ ਹੈ। ਟੈਸਟ ਲਈ, ਤੁਸੀਂ ਸਿਵਲ ਹਸਪਤਾਲ ਬਠਿੰਡਾ ਦੀ ਲੈਬ ਵਿੱਚ ਜਾ ਸਕਦੇ ਹੋ। ਕਿਰਪਾ ਕਰਕੇ ਆਪਣੇ [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]] ਕਾਰਡ ਨਾਲ ਲੈ ਕੇ ਜਾਓ।
ਮਰੀਜ਼ [[PATIENT_NAME|Balwinder Singh]]: ਮੈਂ ਕੱਲ੍ਹ ਜਾਵਾਂਗਾ। ਇੱਕ ਹੋਰ ਗੱਲ, ਡਾਕਟਰ, ਮੈਂ ਆਪਣੀ ਸਿਹਤ ਅਤੇ ਆਪਣੇ ਪਰਿਵਾਰ ਬਾਰੇ ਬਹੁਤ ਚਿੰਤਤ ਹਾਂ। ਜਦੋਂ ਮੈਂ ਚਿੰਤਤ ਹੁੰਦਾ ਹਾਂ ਤਾਂ ਮੈਂ ਮਿਠਾਈਆਂ 'ਤੇ ਬਹੁਤ ਖਰਚ ਕਰਦਾ ਹਾਂ।
ਡਾਕਟਰ [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Harpreet Kaur]]: ਮੈਂ ਤੁਹਾਡੀ ਚਿੰਤਾ ਸਮਝਦਾ ਹਾਂ, ਬਲਵਿੰਦਰ ਜੀ। ਆਪਣੀ ਮਾਨਸਿਕ ਸਿਹਤ ਦਾ ਵੀ ਧਿਆਨ ਰੱਖਣਾ ਮਹੱਤਵਪੂਰਨ ਹੈ। ਆਪਣੇ ਇਲਾਜ 'ਤੇ ਧਿਆਨ ਦਿਓ। ਅਸੀਂ ਤੁਹਾਡਾ ਸਮਰਥਨ ਕਰਾਂਗੇ। ਕਿਰਪਾ ਕਰਕੇ ਮੈਨੂੰ ਆਪਣੇ ਟੈਸਟ ਦੇ ਨਤੀਜਿਆਂ ਬਾਰੇ ਅਪਡੇਟ ਦਿੰਦੇ ਰਹੋ।
ਮਰੀਜ਼ [[PATIENT_NAME|Balwinder Singh]]: ਧੰਨਵਾਦ, ਡਾਕਟਰ। ਮੈਂ ਕਰਾਂਗਾ।

persona: Preeti Kaur, a 55-year-old culinary and craft guardian, blends traditional Punjabi flavors and phulkari art with a skeptical, emotionally intense streak that flares up when modern shortcuts or music cuts off unexpectedly.
cultural_background: Preeti Kaur hails from a Punjabi Sikh family rooted in the agricultural rhythms of Muktsar, a rural town in the heart of Punjab. Her upbringing is steeped in vibrant folk traditions—Baisakhi celebrations, Punjabi folk songs, and the Sikh values of seva, humility, and community solidarity. The daily life of her village, with its fields, cattle, and extended families, has shaped her practical outlook while also leaving room for her curious mind to explore new household strategies.
linguistic_background: Her mother tongue is Punjabi, which she speaks fluently and uses for all daily interactions. Because she never attended school, she is illiterate in both the Gurmukhi script used for Punjabi and the Devanagari script for Hindi; she cannot read or write in any language. She can understand spoken Hindi well enough to follow simple instructions and chat with neighbours, but all her communication is oral.
skills_and_expertise: Through decades of managing her home, Preeti has honed a range of practical skills. She excels at preparing traditional Punjabi dishes—such as sarson da saag, makki di roti, dal makhni, and festive sweets—for family and communal meals. She is a skilled handloom phulkari embroiderer, creating vibrant floral motifs on cloths used in weddings and festivals. She maintains a modest kitchen garden that supplies seasonal vegetables, and she looks after a small flock of dairy goats, providing fresh milk for daily use. She is proficient in leading oral recitations of Gurbani during family prayers and can guide the performance of Sikh rites despite her illiteracy. Additionally, she efficiently handles cash‑based household budgeting and is comfortable organising Langar for community gatherings. She is skeptical of modern kitchen appliances, preferring traditional wooden tools and the proven techniques of her ancestors, and she can become emotionally reactive if a cooking mishap leads to burnt food.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Traditional Punjabi cooking", "Phulkari (handloom embroidery)", "Small‑scale vegetable gardening", "Dairy goat husbandry", "Sikh devotional singing (Kirtan)", "Cash‑based household budgeting", "Organising Langar events"]
hobbies_and_interests: She enjoys listening to Punjabi folk music such as bhangra beats and boliyan, especially during evenings on the radio. She likes knitting scarves and small shawls for the winter months. She spends time maintaining her kitchen garden, growing seasonal vegetables like bitter gourd and coriander. Attending Gurdwara services and participating in community Langar as a volunteer bring her spiritual satisfaction. She also loves taking part in village kite‑flying festivals during the windy winter, collecting and reciting old Punjabi proverbs, and strolling to the choupal for casual gossip with neighbours. When her favorite bhangra track ends abruptly, she can feel a surge of disappointment, reflecting her high emotional reactivity.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Listening to Punjabi folk music", "Knit scarves", "Maintaining a kitchen garden", "Attending Gurdwara services", "Participating in village kite‑flying festivals", "Collecting and reciting Punjabi proverbs", "Walking to the choupal for gossip"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Preeti Kaur wishes to ensure that the rich culinary and craft traditions of her Punjabi Sikh heritage are passed down to the next generation. She hopes to mentor her grandchildren and other village girls in preparing classic Punjabi dishes and creating phulkari work, perhaps through informal weekend workshops held at her home. She also aspires to start a modest home‑based venture selling homemade pickles, traditional sweets, and embroidered items at the weekly market, providing a small but steady supplementary income. Recognising her anxiety, she aims to deepen her spiritual practice—regular meditation on Gurbani and increased seva at the local Gurdwara—to help manage emotional stress. While she prefers flexible, unstructured schedules, she is keen to see her family and community benefit from her practical knowledge.

Her background suggests limited formal health literacy, which may impact her ability to navigate complex medical information.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):

user_prompt:
Write one complete Telemedicine / Doctor-Patient Chat Transcript for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Punjabi (code=pa, script=Gurmukhi). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

PLACEMENT (device/network): Put IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, and MAC_ADDRESS exactly once each in a short session/metadata header. Do NOT repeat them in chat turns. Chat turns use patient/doctor/phone/email/appointment only.
Also put AGE and GENDER exactly once in the session header (e.g. Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|34]] [[GENDER|Female]]).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 55
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Punjab
- district: Muktsar
- first_language: Punjabi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 55-year-old female from rural Punjab, currently married, with no formal occupation and illiterate education, speaks Punjabi as her first language. She presents with lifestyle cues consistent with a traditional, home-based lifestyle, likely involving domestic activities and community engagement. Her background suggests limited formal health literacy, which may impact her ability to navigate complex medical information.

Document format guide for Telemedicine / Doctor-Patient Chat Transcript (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton — put device/network IDs ONLY in a short session header (once each),
then keep chat turns free of IP/IMEI/MAC/URL spam:
--- session ---
Appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|...]] Portal [[URL|https://tele.example.in/visit]]
Client IP [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]] Device IMEI [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
MAC [[MAC_ADDRESS|A1:B2:C3:D4:E5:F6]]
--- chat ---
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|34]] [[GENDER|Female]] ([[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]], [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|...]]): <symptom>
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]] ([[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]): <advice>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- EMAIL_ADDRESS — Email Address e.g. [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
- APPOINTMENT_ID — Appointment ID e.g. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
- IP_ADDRESS — IP Address e.g. [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
- URL — URL (Personal Website/Profile) e.g. [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
- IMEI_NUMBER — IMEI Number e.g. [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
- MAC_ADDRESS — MAC Address e.g. [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ਮਰੀਜ਼ [[PATIENT_NAME|Jaswinder Kaur]] [[AGE|55]] [[GENDER|Female]] ([[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]], [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|jaswinder.kaur@example.com]]): ਸਤਿ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਅਕਾਲ ਡਾਕਟਰ ਸਾਹਿਬ, ਮੈਂ ਪਿਛਲੇ ਦੋ ਦਿਨਾਂ ਤੋਂ ਆਪਣੇ ਸੱਜੇ ਪਾਸੇ ਦਰਦ ਮਹਿਸੂਸ ਕਰ ਰਿਹਾ ਹਾਂ। ਇਹ ਤੇਜ਼ ਦਰਦ ਹੈ, ਅਤੇ ਜਦੋਂ ਮੈਂ ਖੰਘਦਾ ਹਾਂ ਜਾਂ ਹਿੱਲਦਾ ਹਾਂ ਤਾਂ ਦਰਦ ਹੁੰਦਾ ਹੈ। ਮੈਨੂੰ ਚਿੰਤਾ ਹੈ ਕਿ ਸ਼ਾਇਦ ਇਹ ਕੋਈ ਗੰਭੀਰ ਗੱਲ ਹੋਵੇ।
ਡਾਕਟਰ [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Gurpreet Singh]] ([[HOSPITAL_NAME|Muktsar Civil Hospital]]): ਨਮਸਤਿ ਜਸਵਿੰਦਰ ਜੀ, ਮੈਂ ਤੁਹਾਡੀ ਚਿੰਤਾ ਸਮਝਦਾ ਹਾਂ। ਕੀ ਤੁਸੀਂ ਦਰਦ ਬਾਰੇ ਥੋੜ੍ਹਾ ਹੋਰ ਦੱਸ ਸਕਦੇ ਹੋ? ਕੀ ਇਹ ਲਗਾਤਾਰ ਦਰਦ ਹੈ ਜਾਂ ਆਉਂਦਾ-ਜਾਂਦਾ ਰਹਿੰਦਾ ਹੈ? ਨਾਲ ਹੀ, ਕੀ ਤੁਹਾਨੂੰ ਬੁਖਾਰ ਹੈ ਜਾਂ ਜੀਅ ਕੱਚਾ ਮਹਿਸੂਸ ਹੁੰਦਾ ਹੈ?
ਮਰੀਜ਼ [[PATIENT_NAME|Jaswinder Kaur]]: ਦਰਦ ਜ਼ਿਆਦਾਤਰ ਲਗਾਤਾਰ ਰਹਿੰਦਾ ਹੈ, ਪਰ ਜਦੋਂ ਮੈਂ ਬੈਠਣ ਤੋਂ ਖੜ੍ਹੇ ਹੋਣ ਦੀ ਕੋਸ਼ਿਸ਼ ਕਰਦਾ ਹਾਂ ਤਾਂ ਇਹ ਹੋਰ ਵੱਧ ਜਾਂਦਾ ਹੈ। ਮੈਨੂੰ ਬੁਖਾਰ ਨਹੀਂ ਹੈ, ਪਰ ਮੈਨੂੰ ਥੋੜ੍ਹੀ ਕਮਜ਼ੋਰੀ ਮਹਿਸੂਸ ਹੋ ਰਹੀ ਹੈ ਅਤੇ ਅੱਜ ਮੈਂ ਜ਼ਿਆਦਾ ਕੁਝ ਨਹੀਂ ਖਾਧਾ।
ਡਾਕਟਰ [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Gurpreet Singh]]: ਜਾਣਕਾਰੀ ਲਈ ਧੰਨਵਾਦ। ਜੋ ਤੁਸੀਂ ਕਹਿ ਰਹੇ ਹੋ, ਉਸ ਤੋਂ ਲੱਗਦਾ ਹੈ ਕਿ ਇਹ ਮਾਸਪੇਸ਼ੀਆਂ ਦਾ ਖਿਚਾਅ ਜਾਂ ਗੁਰਦੇ ਦੀ ਪੱਥਰੀ ਹੋ ਸਕਦੀ ਹੈ। ਕਿਉਂਕਿ ਤੁਸੀਂ ਪੇਂਡੂ ਪੰਜਾਬ ਵਿੱਚ ਹੋ, ਮੈਨੂੰ ਇਹ ਯਕੀਨੀ ਬਣਾਉਣਾ ਪਵੇਗਾ ਕਿ ਜੇ ਲੋੜ ਪਈ ਤਾਂ ਤੁਸੀਂ ਹਸਪਤਾਲ ਪਹੁੰਚ ਸਕੋ। ਮੈਂ ਤੁਹਾਡੇ ਲਈ ਸਿਵਲ ਹਸਪਤਾਲ ਮੁਕਤਸਰ ਜਾਣ ਲਈ ਇੱਕ ਐਂਬੂਲੈਂਸ ਦਾ ਪ੍ਰਬੰਧ ਕਰ ਰਿਹਾ ਹਾਂ। ਉਹ ਪੱਥਰੀ ਜਾਂ ਹੋਰ ਸਮੱਸਿਆਵਾਂ ਦੀ ਜਾਂਚ ਕਰਨ ਲਈ ਅਲਟਰਾਸਾਊਂਡ ਕਰਨਗੇ। ਕਿਰਪਾ ਕਰਕੇ ਜਾਂਚ ਹੋਣ ਤੱਕ ਕੁਝ ਵੀ ਨਾ ਖਾਓ ਜਾਂ ਪੀਓ।
ਮਰੀਜ਼ [[PATIENT_NAME|Jaswinder Kaur]]: ਇੱਕ ਐਂਬੂਲੈਂਸ? ਠੀਕ ਹੈ ਡਾਕਟਰ। ਮੈਂ ਥੋੜ੍ਹਾ ਡਰਿਆ ਹੋਇਆ ਹਾਂ, ਪਰ ਮੈਂ ਉਵੇਂ ਹੀ ਕਰਾਂਗਾ ਜਿਵੇਂ ਤੁਸੀਂ ਕਹੋਗੇ। ਮੈਨੂੰ ਆਪਣੇ ਪਤੀ ਨੂੰ ਕੀ ਦੱਸਣਾ ਚਾਹੀਦਾ ਹੈ?
ਡਾਕਟਰ [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Gurpreet Singh]]: ਸੁਰੱਖਿਅਤ ਰਹਿਣਾ ਬਿਹਤਰ ਹੈ। ਕਿਰਪਾ ਕਰਕੇ ਆਪਣੇ ਪਤੀ ਨੂੰ ਦੱਸੋ ਕਿ ਮੈਂ ਤੁਹਾਨੂੰ ਜਾਂਚ ਲਈ ਹਸਪਤਾਲ ਜਾਣ ਦੀ ਸਲਾਹ ਦਿੱਤੀ ਹੈ। ਐਂਬੂਲੈਂਸ ਜਲਦੀ ਹੀ ਉੱਥੇ ਪਹੁੰਚ ਜਾਵੇਗੀ। ਇੱਕ ਨਰਸ ਤੁਹਾਡੇ ਫੋਨ 'ਤੇ ਕਾਲ ਕਰੇਗੀ ਜਦੋਂ ਉਹ ਪਹੁੰਚਣਗੇ। ਤੁਸੀਂ ਸੁਰੱਖਿਅਤ ਹੱਥਾਂ ਵਿੱਚ ਹੋ।

Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Jaswinder Kaur]] [[AGE|55]] [[GENDER|Female]] ([[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]], [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|jaswinder.kaur@example.com]]): Namaste doctor saheb, I am having pain in my right side for the last two days. It is a sharp pain, and it hurts when I cough or move. I am worried it might be something serious.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Gurpreet Singh]] ([[HOSPITAL_NAME|Muktsar Civil Hospital]]): Hello Jaswinder ji, I understand your concern. Can you please describe the pain a little more? Is it a constant pain or does it come and go? Also, do you have any fever or feel nauseous?
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Jaswinder Kaur]]: The pain is mostly constant, but it gets worse when I try to stand up from sitting. I don't have a fever, but I feel a bit weak and have not eaten much today.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Gurpreet Singh]]: Thank you for the details. Based on what you are saying, it sounds like it could be a muscle strain or possibly a kidney stone. Since you are in rural Punjab, I need to ensure you can get to a hospital if needed. I am arranging for an ambulance to take you to the Civil Hospital in Muktsar. They will do an ultrasound to check for any stones or other issues. Please do not eat or drink anything until you are examined.
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Jaswinder Kaur]]: An ambulance? Okay, doctor. I am a bit scared, but I will do as you say. What should I tell my husband?
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Gurpreet Singh]]: It is better to be safe. Please tell your husband that I have advised you to go to the hospital for a check-up. The ambulance will be there shortly. A nurse will call you on your phone before they arrive. You are in safe hands.

linguistic_persona: Esther Mary is a native Tamil speaker; they read and write fluently in Tamil, possess functional English for workplace instructions, and can recognize a handful of Hindi words from occasional media exposure.
sports_persona: Esther Mary is an avid cricket fan who regularly watches matches, especially supporting the Chennai Super Kings, and enjoys occasional morning walks along the coastal promenade, though they rarely participate in organized sports.
arts_persona: Esther Mary engages with the arts by singing devotional hymns in the church choir, enjoying Tamil cinema featuring actors Vijay and Nayanthara, listening to A.R. Rahman’s melodies, and reading novels by author Sujatha, which together enrich their cultural outlook.
travel_persona: Esther Mary prefers short road trips to nearby coastal towns like Kanyakumari and historic sites such as the Mahabalipuram temples, often planning family outings with a modest budget, while dreaming of a future visit to Sri Lanka’s cultural heartland.
culinary_persona: Esther Mary delights in preparing traditional Tamil vegetarian dishes such as dosa, idli, sambar, rasam, and pongal, favoring ingredients like tamarind, curry leaves, and mustard seeds, and they often experiment with new spice blends for community feasts.
persona: Esther Mary, a 38‑year‑old abrasive‑slitting specialist, blends meticulous craftsmanship with a love for community choir, Tamil vegetarian cooking, and cricket, while juggling ambitious career goals, prudent finances, and a habit of over‑stocking spices in their kitchen.
cultural_background: Esther Mary is part of the Tamil Christian community in Thoothukkudi, a coastal town in Tamil Nadu. The region blends a traditional agrarian and fishing lifestyle with a modest industrial presence where she works. Her family observes Tamil cultural practices such as Pongal, respect for elders, and community gatherings, while also celebrating Christian festivals like Christmas, Easter, and Sunday worship. Growing up in a rural setting, she internalises the collective values of modesty, hard work, and hospitality, infused with the occasional exposure to modern manufacturing influences.
linguistic_background: Esther’s mother tongue is Tamil, which she reads, writes, and speaks fluently. She completed primary school in a Tamil‑medium government school, giving her functional literacy and basic numeracy. While she has limited formal training in English, she can understand simple workplace instructions in English and communicate essential information with her coworkers. She has no formal knowledge of Hindi, but occasional exposure to Hindi media in the region allows her to recognize a few common words.
skills_and_expertise: Esther is a proficient operator of abrasive slitting machines, adept at setting up the equipment, adjusting feed rates, and monitoring blade wear. She follows strict safety protocols, conducts routine inspections, and performs basic maintenance tasks such as lubrication and part replacement. Her meticulous attention to detail ensures high‑quality output with minimal defects. Additionally, she is skilled in record‑keeping, maintaining production logs, and coordinating with the quality control team to address any issues promptly.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Abrasive slitting machine operation", "Equipment setup and adjustment", "Feed rate optimization", "Blade wear monitoring", "Safety protocol adherence", "Routine equipment inspection", "Basic machine maintenance (lubrication, part replacement)", "Quality assurance coordination", "Production log maintenance", "Team communication and coordination"]
hobbies_and_interests: Esther enjoys singing in the church choir, cooking traditional Tamil vegetarian meals, tending a small kitchen garden, reading devotional books and Tamil novels, watching Tamil movies, taking part in community festivals and cultural events, casually watching cricket matches with friends, and doing DIY home improvement projects during her free time. Her sociable nature makes her a regular participant in group activities, and her curiosity drives her to explore new recipes and crafts.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Church choir singing", "Cooking traditional Tamil vegetarian dishes", "Gardening and home vegetable cultivation", "Reading devotional literature and Tamil novels", "Watching Tamil cinema", "Participating in community festivals and cultural events", "Casual cricket match viewing", "DIY home improvement projects"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Esther aims to deepen her technical knowledge by enrolling in short‑term training programmes on advanced abrasive slitting technology, safety management, and lean manufacturing. Over the next three to five years, she hopes to be promoted to senior operator or shift supervisor, leading a small team and driving process improvements. Simultaneously, she wishes to secure a stable financial future for her family, ensuring her children's higher education and eventually starting a modest home‑based tailoring or crafts business, leveraging her organizational skills and community network.
persona_clinical_summary: A 38‑year‑old female from Thoothukkudi, Tamil Nadu, works as a primary‑educated abrasive‑slitting machine operator in a rural setting. She is married, speaks Tamil, and maintains a vegetarian diet. Her lifestyle includes community choir participation and modest financial planning.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):

user_prompt:
Write one complete Prescription for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Tamil (code=ta, script=Tamil). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 38
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Slitting Machine Operator (Abrasive)
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Rural
- state: Tamil Nadu
- district: Thoothukkudi
- first_language: Tamil
- clinical_persona_summary: A 38‑year‑old female from Thoothukkudi, Tamil Nadu, works as a primary‑educated abrasive‑slitting machine operator in a rural setting. She is married, speaks Tamil, and maintains a vegetarian diet. Her lifestyle includes community choir participation and modest financial planning.

Document format guide for Prescription (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Prescription — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|28]] / [[GENDER|Male]], MRN [[MRN|RX-1001]]
Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Rx: <drug dose frequency>  (do NOT tag drug names as entities)

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS — Residential Address e.g. [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once (parking/ambulance or motor claim line). Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):

She is a homemaker managing household budgeting and community care, fluent in Tamil with basic English skills. Her lifestyle includes daily yoga for anxiety management and traditional cooking of regional dishes.

Document format guide for Insurance Claim / TPA Form (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):

MANDATORY inline entities (16 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:

- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]

நோயாளி 15 நவம்பர் 2024 அன்று நுரையீரல் காசநோயுடன் அனுமதிக்கப்பட்டார். ஆரம்பகால நோயறிதல் சளி கறை நுண்ணோக்கி மற்றும் GeneXpert MTB/RIF மதிப்பீடு மூலம் உறுதி செய்யப்பட்டது. நோயாளி 45 வயதுடைய, கிராமப்புற ஈரோடு, தமிழ்நாடு, திருமணமான, தொடக்கக் கல்வி பயின்ற பெண்மணி. நோயாளிக்கு மூன்று வாரங்களாக தொடர்ச்சியான இருமல், குறைந்த காய்ச்சல், இரவு நேர வியர்வை மற்றும் சுமார் 5 கிலோ எடை குறைவு போன்ற அறிகுறிகள் தென்படுகின்றன. மார்பு எக்ஸ்-ரே வலது மேல் மடலில் ஊடுருவல்கள் இருப்பதைக் காட்டுகிறது. சளி மாதிரி வளர்ப்பு மற்றும் உணர்திறன் சோதனைக்காக அனுப்பப்பட்டது. நோயாளி திருத்தப்பட்ட தேசிய காசநோய் கட்டுப்பாட்டுத் திட்டத்தின் (RNTCP) கீழ் தரப்படுத்தப்பட்ட காசநோய் எதிர்ப்பு சிகிச்சையை (ATT) தொடங்கினார். அடிப்படை கல்லீரல் செயல்பாடு மற்றும் சிறுநீரக செயல்பாடு சோதனைகள் இயல்பான வரம்பிற்குள் இருந்தன. நோயாளிக்கு சிகிச்சையைத் தொடர்வது மற்றும் ஊட்டச்சத்து குறித்து ஆலோசனைகள் வழங்கப்பட்டன. சிகிச்சைக்குப் பிறகு இரண்டு மாதங்களில் பின்தொடர் சளி பரிசோதனை திட்டமிடப்பட்டுள்ளது. அறியப்பட்ட பிற நோய்கள் ஏதுமில்லை. குடும்ப வரலாறு குறிப்பிடத்தக்கது அல்ல. தொழில் homemaker எனப் பட்டியலிடப்பட்டுள்ளது. காசநோயால் பாதிக்கப்பட்டவர்களுடன் தொழில்முறைத் தொடர்பு ஏதுமில்லை. நோயாளி ஈரோடு அருகே உள்ள ஒரு கிராமத்தில் வசிக்கிறார். உள்ளூர் ஆரம்ப சுகாதார நிலையத்திலிருந்து பரிந்துரைக்கப்பட்டார். கடந்த 5 ஆண்டுகளில் மருத்துவமனையில் அனுமதிக்கப்பட்ட வரலாறு இல்லை. காப்பீட்டு வசதி சரிபார்க்கப்பட்டது. விவரமான பில்களின் அடிப்படையில் கோரிக்கை தொகை மதிப்பிடப்பட்டது. ஆம்புலன்ஸ் போக்குவரத்து கோரிக்கைக்காக வாகனப் பதிவு வழங்கப்பட்டது. கோரிக்கைத் தொகையைச் செலுத்துவதற்காக வங்கி விவரங்கள் சரிபார்க்கப்பட்டன.

Patient admitted on 15 November 2024 with pulmonary tuberculosis. Initial diagnosis confirmed by sputum smear microscopy and GeneXpert MTB/RIF assay. Patient is a 45-year-old female homemaker from rural Erode, Tamil Nadu, married, with primary education. She presents with persistent cough for three weeks, low-grade fever, night sweats, and weight loss of approximately 5 kg. Chest X-ray shows infiltrates in the right upper lobe. Sputum sample sent for culture and sensitivity. Patient started on standard anti-tubercular therapy (ATT) under Revised National Tuberculosis Control Programme (RNTCP) regimen. Baseline liver function tests and renal function tests were within normal limits. Patient counseled on treatment adherence and nutrition. Follow-up sputum examination planned after two months of treatment. No known comorbidities. Family history non-contributory. Occupation listed as homemaker. No occupational exposure to TB. Patient resides in a village near Erode. Referred from a local primary health centre. No prior hospitalizations in the last 5 years. Insurance coverage verified. Claim amount estimated based on itemized bills. Vehicle registration provided for ambulance transport claim. Bank details verified for claim settlement.
judge_reasoning: Tamil prose dominant with allowed Latin medical/lab terms and ID tags; all TYPEs in allow-list; 45yo female TB case fits persona; no mismatches or violations.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Radiology / Imaging Report for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Telugu (code=te, script=Telugu). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 32
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Track Repairer (Rail Transport)
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Rural
- state: Andhra Pradesh
- district: Sri Potti Sriramulu Nellore
- first_language: Telugu
- clinical_persona_summary: A 32-year-old male from Sri Potti Sriramulu Nellore, Andhra Pradesh, works as a track repairer in rail transport, with primary education and a rural background. He is never married, speaks Telugu as his first language, and leads a lifestyle that includes occasional participation in village cricket and a diet rich in traditional foods like ragi mudde and pulihora. His clinical profile reflects a context of limited formal education and a rural occupation, with potential for occupational hazards related to track maintenance.

Document format guide for Radiology / Imaging Report (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Imaging report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|45]] [[GENDER|Female]]
MRN [[MRN|...]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|...]] | Findings: <radiology prose, untagged>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- ENCOUNTER_ID — Encounter / Visit ID e.g. [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] ఎన్‌కౌంటర్ [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]] | ఫలితాలు: రోగికి ఎడమ చేతి ఎక్స్-రే తీయబడింది. ఎక్స్-రేలో ఎడమ ఐదవ మెటాకార్పల్ షాఫ్ట్ యొక్క స్థానభ్రంశం లేని పగులు స్పష్టంగా కనిపిస్తుంది. కీళ్లలో ఎటువంటి ప్రమేయం లేదా అమరిక లోపం ఉన్నట్లు ఆధారాలు లేవు. మిగిలిన కార్పల్ ఎముకలు, ఫాలాంజెస్‌ మరియు డిస్టల్ రేడియస్/అల్నా సాధారణంగా ఉన్నాయి. పగులు ఉన్న ప్రదేశానికి సమీపంలో మృదు కణజాల వాపు ఉంది. అభిప్రాయం: ఎడమ ఐదవ మెటాకార్పల్ షాఫ్ట్ యొక్క స్థానభ్రంశం లేని పగులు. సిఫార్సు: 4-6 వారాల పాటు షార్ట్ ఆర్మ్ కాస్ట్‌లో స్థిరీకరించాలి మరియు ఆర్థోపెడిక్ క్లినిక్‌లో తదుపరి పరీక్షలకు రావాలి. ఈ ప్రక్రియను డాక్టర్ [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Srinivas Reddy]] నిర్వహించారు. రోగి యొక్క ABHA ID [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]. రోగి [[VILLAGE|Kandukur]] గ్రామంలో, [[DISTRICT|Sri Potti Sriramulu Nellore]], [[STATE|Andhra Pradesh]] నివసిస్తున్నారు. అతని సంప్రదింపు నంబర్ [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]. ఈ రిపోర్ట్ [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Nellore]], [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-AP-005]] నుండి రూపొందించబడింది.

linguistic_persona: Khalil Ravu speaks Telugu fluently as their mother tongue, commands English with technical precision thanks to their engineering education, and can read simple Hindi signs, though they rarely use Hindi in daily communication.
sports_persona: Khalil Ravu is a passionate weekend cricketer who captains a local street-batting team, avidly follows Sunrisers Hyderabad in the IPL, and they maintain fitness through regular morning jogs and occasional gym sessions, though they sometimes neglect stretching after matches.
arts_persona: Khalil Ravu frequently visits modern art galleries to admire works by artists like Tyeb Mehta and Anjolie Elahee, relishes Carnatic ragas on the harmonium, especially compositions of M. Balamuralikrishna, and they capture everyday life through street photography inspired by Raghu Rai.
travel_persona: Khalil Ravu enjoys weekend getaways to nearby historical sites like Hampi and the coastal beaches of Goa, they plan trips meticulously using travel apps, but they also embrace spontaneous weekend drives to the countryside, while dreaming of a future cultural tour of Istanbul and Marrakech.
culinary_persona: Khalil Ravu delights in cooking fragrant Hyderabadi biryani with basmati rice, saffron, and locally sourced goat meat, crafts tangy mango pickles using raw mangoes and mustard seeds, and they relish tasting street-food samosas, though they often rely on ready-made spice mixes for convenience.
persona: Khalil Ravu blends meticulous school inspections with a love for art, cricket, and biryani, and they balance disciplined finances with occasional gadget splurges, staying endlessly talkative and community-oriented.
cultural_background: Born into a Telugu‑speaking Muslim family in Mahbubnagar, an urban centre of Telangana, Khalil Ravu grew up at the intersection of Deccani Muslim traditions and the modern, technology‑focused rhythm of his city. His household observed the festivals of Eid and Ramadan while also participating in regional customs such as Bathukamma and local Telugu literary gatherings. This blend of southern Indian urban culture and Islamic communal values shaped his worldview, fostering a deep respect for education, community harmony, and cultural diversity.
linguistic_background: Telugu is Khalil’s mother tongue, and he is fully literate in reading, writing, and speaking it. He completed his engineering degree in an English‑medium college, giving him excellent proficiency in English for technical, administrative, and interpersonal communication. While Hindi is taught in schools across India, he has only a basic ability to read simple signs and does not use Hindi regularly in his professional or personal life.
skills_and_expertise: Khalil combines strong technical know‑how with deep educational insight. His engineering background equips him with data analysis, statistical reporting, and the ability to implement digital tools for school management. As a school inspector, he is skilled in conducting thorough inspections, evaluating compliance with curriculum standards, and drafting comprehensive audit reports. He excels in stakeholder communication, conflict resolution, and capacity‑building workshops for teachers. His passion for the arts also enables him to assess extracurricular programs and promote cultural activities within schools.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Data Analysis", "Statistical Reporting", "Digital School Management", "School Inspection", "Curriculum Compliance Evaluation", "Audit Report Writing", "Stakeholder Communication", "Conflict Resolution", "Teacher Capacity Building", "Arts Program Assessment", "Cultural Activity Promotion", "Engineering Problem-Solving"]
hobbies_and_interests: Khalil enjoys a vibrant mix of cultural and recreational activities. He frequently visits modern art galleries and museums to explore contemporary visual art, and he listens to Carnatic classical music while playing the harmonium. He reads extensively on educational innovation, sociology, and philosophy, keeping his curiosity alive. On weekends, he plays cricket with friends at the local ground, and he captures moments of everyday life through street photography. He also loves experimenting with traditional Telugu recipes, especially biryani and pickles, and takes part in community festivals, Eid gatherings, and local fairs where he can meet people and exchange ideas.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Visiting modern art galleries and museums", "Listening to Carnatic classical music", "Reading books on educational innovation, sociology, and philosophy", "Playing cricket with friends", "Street photography", "Cooking traditional Telugu dishes (e.g., biryani, pickles)", "Participating in community festivals, Eid gatherings, and local fairs"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Khalil’s ambition is to advance to a senior leadership role within the Telangana education department, such as Deputy Director of Education, where he can drive large‑scale reforms in school quality and digital integration. He plans to pursue a postgraduate diploma in educational leadership within the next two years, and to spearhead a mentorship program that supports junior inspectors and teachers, especially from marginalized Muslim and minority backgrounds. His long‑term goal is to create a model of inclusive, technology‑enabled schooling that can be replicated across urban and rural districts of the state.
persona_clinical_summary: A 36-year-old male school inspector from Telangana, currently married, with a graduate education working in an urban district. He is a Telugu-speaking Muslim who completed engineering in an English-medium college and is proficient in both languages. His lifestyle includes regular physical activity, occasional gadget purchases, and a preference for home-cooked meals.

MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] ఎన్కౌంటర్ [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]] | ఫలితాలు: రోగి గత మూడు వారాలుగా నిరంతర దగ్గు మరియు తక్కువ స్థాయి జ్వరంతో వచ్చారు. ఛాతీ రేడియోగ్రాఫ్ నిర్వహించబడింది. ఊపిరితిత్తుల క్షేత్రాలు కుడి ఎగువ లోబ్లో సుమారు 3.5 సెం.మీ. గరిష్ట పరిమాణంలో స్పష్టమైన కావిటరీ గాయాన్ని చూపుతాయి. కావిటీ గోడ మందంగా మరియు క్రమరహితంగా కనిపిస్తుంది. దీనికి సంబంధించిన చుట్టూ ఉన్న కన్సాలిడేషన్ మరియు చిన్న కుడి ప్లూరల్ ఎఫ్యూషన్ ఉన్నాయి. గణనీయమైన హీలర్ లింఫాడెనోపతి గమనించబడలేదు. ఈ ఫలితాలు యాక్టివ్ పల్మనరీ ట్యూబర్‌క్యులోసిస్ను సూచిస్తున్నాయి. రోగి యొక్క [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]] ఈ రికార్డుతో అనుసంధానించబడింది. ఈ నివేదిక [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Kumar]] ద్వారా నిర్దేశించబడింది. ఫాలో-అప్ కోసం [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] వద్ద సంప్రదించవచ్చు. రోగి [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-TG-001]] కింద అడ్మిట్ చేయబడ్డారు. రోగి [[DISTRICT|Mahbubnagar]] లో నివసిస్తున్నారు.

Imaging report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Mahbubnagar District Hospital]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|Ahmed Khan]] [[AGE|36]] [[GENDER|Male]]
MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]] | Findings: The patient presented with a persistent cough and low-grade fever for the past three weeks. A chest radiograph was performed. The lung fields show a well-defined, cavitary lesion in the right upper lobe, measuring approximately 3.5 cm in its greatest dimension. The cavity wall appears thick and irregular. There is associated surrounding consolidation and a small right pleural effusion. No significant hilar lymphadenopathy is noted. The findings are suggestive of active pulmonary tuberculosis. The patient's [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]] is linked to this record. The report was dictated by [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Kumar]]. Contact for follow-up can be made at [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]. The patient is admitted under [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-TG-001]]. The patient resides in [[DISTRICT|Mahbubnagar]].
